stimulus: Acacia senegal, a tree found in the dry savannas of West Africa, is the world's main source of natural gum arabic, a substance used in foods, paints, and inks. The tree produces a sticky sap that hardens into the gum. The branches of Acacia senegal are helpful for the process of harvesting gum arabic because they have a distinctive feature that lets harvesters collect the sap easily. A pattern of weak points runs along each branch, and when harvesters peel back small strips of bark there, the sap flows out steadily and pools without much waste.
stem: What feature of Acacia senegal does the text say is helpful for the process of harvesting gum arabic?

answerOptions.content: It can grow in a wide range of climates around the world.
Its sap produces gum arabic of an especially high purity.
Its branches have weak points that make it easy to collect the sap.
It is one of only two trees in West Africa that produce sticky sap.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because the text states that the branches' pattern of weak points lets harvesters peel back strips of bark and collect the sap easily.

Choice A is incorrect; the text mentions only the dry savannas of West Africa, not a wide range of climates. Choice B is incorrect; the text does not discuss the purity of gum arabic. Choice D is incorrect; the text says Acacia senegal is the main source, not that it is one of only two sources.
